scores.metric: sandbox --disable all (masked Levenshtein); build_insns 107 vs target 108 (1 short = lost-codegen insert)

sessions.lever: diagnosis + base-copy alias attempt (s16 *p = s1) — did not materialize the copy

reviewer.evidence: 10 regfix rules (1 lost-codegen insert + 9-rule tail-store subst cluster). No pure-C form found. Two coupled gaps: (A) a folded `s3=a0+3` recompute, (B) a materialized base copy s1->$v0 for the tail stores. The single-set pointer alias gets copy-propagated (s1 dead after), so the copy doesn't materialize.

remaining_gap.summary: Tail store block must emit through a COPY of s1 in $v0 (addu v0,s1,zero) with $v1 scratch (a0+3 then 0x8000); and an extra `addiu s3,s2,3` recompute that GCC folds. build_insns 107 vs target 108. The 9 tail substs + 1 insert encode exactly this.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: All 10 rules are the cheat; HEAD only matches with them. No pure-C lever found that materializes the base copy AND restores the folded recompute.

next_hypotheses: Materialize the s1->$v0 base copy: per param-reuse-base-copy-cse-canon, a single-set alias folds when the original is dead — instead keep s1 live past the copy, OR reuse the PARAMETER as a cursor, OR name the copy as a value GCC must keep. Verify via full build (sandbox build_insns must hit 108 AND registers match — masked sandbox won't show the v0 vs s1 base).

Restore the folded `a0+3` recompute: the top `s3=a0+3` and else `s3=a0 - -3` fold to one. Try a structural change so the else-branch recompute survives (shared-end-label family / distinct value path).
If pure-C plateaus, escalate canonical-asm or document plateau. This is the same hard register/scheduling-cluster class as motion_SetMotion / func_8006156C in this batch.

rejected_forms.form: s16 *p = s1; tail stores via p
rejected_forms.reason: Not a cheat, no effect: GCC copy-propagates the single-set alias (s1 dead after the block), so no `addu v0,s1,zero` materializes. build stayed 107. Need a form that keeps the copy live.
